methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Brad Porter, has raised over $150M to date. The valuation analysis incorporates a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics), supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $500M last post-money valuation as a base, applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yielding $950M. Peer-Median analysis across the 4 core comparables produces a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B. The peer distribution reflects modest clustering around the $1.6B mark, with Bedrock at $1.8B and Nimble at $1.0B representing the outer bounds.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $808M (low), $1.3B (median), and $1.9B (high). The Stage-Progression method anchors the low end, while the Peer-Median method dominates the upper range. Key uncertainties include the comparability of stage-to-stage progression multiples in a dynamic frontier-tech sector and the stability of peer valuations in private markets. The $1.1B spread between low and high reflects these methodological differences and suggests caution around precision at the range extremes.
